stories.title: Fear, defiance, and anger: Iranians describe life under bombardment
stories.description: In messages to NPR, Tehran residents describe largely deserted streets roamed by paramilitary officials and vigilantes. They say security forces are banning gatherings for Nowruz, the Persian new year, this week.

stories.title: Israel and Iran attack gas facilities. And, Cesar Chavez accused of sexual abuse, rape
stories.description: Attacks on gas facilities by Israel and Iran have escalated the war and impacted global markets. And, renowned union leader and labor rights advocate Cesar Chavez is accused of sexual abuse and rape.

stories.title: Iran attacks world's largest liquified natural gas complex in Qatar
stories.description: Iran struck Qatar's massive gas facility after Israel targeted Iran's gas field, putting global energy supplies at risk.

stories.title: What did President Trump know about Iran before going to war
stories.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Nate Swanson, former director for Iran at the National Security Council, about what President Trump understood about Iran before going to war.

stories.title: Japan's prime minister visits the White House under shadow of Iran war
stories.description: Japan Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will be the first U.S. ally to visit the White House since President Trump asked for help in sending ships to patrol the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Why Trump's attacks and threats to Iran's Kharg Island are a big deal
stories.description: If the U.S. follows through on its threats to bomb the oil assets on Kharg Island, the war would likely escalate and oil prices would increase exponentially.

stories.title: Japan's PM to visit Trump as president continues pleas for help with Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: As Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi prepares for her first White House visit, President Donald Trump calls for Japan and other allies to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump threatens to blow up 'entirety' of major Iran gas field if it attacks Qatar again
stories.description: The attacks led to a spike of more than 25% in gas prices in Europe on Thursday.

stories.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

stories.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
stories.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

stories.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
stories.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

stories.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
stories.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

stories.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
stories.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
stories.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

stories.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
stories.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

stories.title: Netanyahu seeks to appease Trump with latest Iran remarks to the press
stories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday denied what he called "fake news" that Israel had dragged US President Donald Trump into war with Iran. "Does anyone really think that someone can tell President Trump what to do?" Netanyahu told journalist at a press conferen...

stories.title: Netanyahu’s Iran war press briefing targeted at ‘audience of one’: Donald Trump
stories.description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu’s press briefing Thursday on the Iran war was targeted at an “audience of one”, says FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky, reporting from Jerusalem, noting that his message was directed at President Donald Trump. The briefing provided a “behind-the-scenes pe...

stories.title: Replay: Israeli PM Netanyahu addresses press conference
stories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday defended Israel’s and the US’s campaign against Iran, saying that airstrikes have largely destroyed Iran’s capacity to enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles. Speaking at a press conference, Netanyahu declared, “We are w...

stories.title: Trump's grounds for concerns: Will the US risk lives to take Iran’s uranium?
stories.description: The Middle East War is on its 19th day, the next phase could well be its most decisive. With a perceived split in US and Israeli strategy now emerging. If we can believe Donald Trump’s words. He says Israel bomned Iran’s gas field “without his knowledge.Then Trump, perhaps playin...

stories.title: 'Idea that Trump can be led around by Israel is foolishness,' ex-envoy to Iran says
stories.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Elliott Abrams, a former special envoy of US President Donald Trump to Iran, dismissed as "foolishness" the idea that Israel led Trump into war with Tehran. Abrams, who served during Trump's first term in office, said he backed the ongoing war, say...

stories.title: Settler violence surges in the West Bank
stories.description: As the Iran-Israel-US-Lebanon war makes headlines around the world, what is happening in the occupied West Bank? Expropriation of Palestinian land, settler attacks and killings have long been the story of the West Bank, but human rights campaigners and experts say that amid the f...

stories.title: 'Disaster: Attacks on gas infrastructure, in Iran and Qatar, may set us back several years'
stories.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Adi Imsirovic, Author, Director at Surrey Clean Energy and Lecturer on Energy Systems at Oxford University. According to Dr.Imsirovic, we are witnessing a structural rupture in the norms that have quietly governed conflict in energy-producin...

stories.title: Six nations say ready to boost 'efforts to ensure safe passage' in Hormuz
stories.description: Six Western allies, including Britain, France, Germany and Japan, said in a joint statement Thursday they were ready "to contribute to appropriate efforts to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz". The statement came as around 20,000 seafarers remained stuck on approxi...

stories.title: 'Not our war': Palestinians mourn first dead after Iran missile fire
stories.description: The blast struck without warning in the Palestinian town of Beit Awa, sending a hail of missile fragments through a beauty salon and killing four women inside. In the Israeli-occupied West Bank, the first Palestinian deaths in the Middle East war have sharpened a sense of helples...

stories.title: Middle East war escalates after attack on key Iranian gas field
stories.description: #Israel hit Iranian's part of the South Pars #gas field, the world's largest offshore reservoir #Tehran shares with #Doha, on March 18. In retaliation for the strikes, #Iran ramped up its attacks on Gulf countries' energy infrastructures, sending #oil prices soaring. Israel also ...

stories.title: Steven Everts: For Europeans, war on Iran 'a diversion' and 'giant gift to Putin'
stories.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Steven Everts, Director of European Union Institute for Security Studies (EUISS). According to Mr. Everts, the current conflict is an unnecessary war defined by cycles of retaliation that risk expanding beyond their original scope, with serious ...

stories.title: REPLAY: Pentagon press conference on war in Middle East
stories.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday there is no "time frame" for ending the US-Israeli war against Iran, which was launched three weeks ago. "We wouldn't want to set a definitive time frame," Hegseth told a news conference, adding that "we're very much on track" and t...

stories.title: Energy prices soar, Iran and US trade threats after Qatar gas hit
stories.description: Oil and gas prices soared Thursday after Iran hit the world's largest liquefied natural gas (LNG) facility in Qatar and threatened to destroy the region's energy infrastructure. Donald Trump, whose country started the war alongside Israel with their attack on Iran on February 28,...

stories.title: US approves $16.5bn arms deal to Gulf states amid rising Iran tensions
stories.description: The proposed deal includes the sale of drones, missiles and radars for Gulf allies, including UAE, Kuwait and Jordan.

stories.title: Why the US Homeland Security shutdown is raising fears of airport delays
stories.description: Hundreds of TSA workers have resigned over unpaid wages, as Congress weighs funding the Department of Homeland Security.

stories.title: Iranians shop for Persian New Year essentials despite war
stories.description: Iranians are shopping for essentials to celebrate the beginning of the Persian New Year, Nowruz, despite the war.

stories.title: Smoke rises after Iranian missile attack on Israel oil refinery in Haifa
stories.description: An Iranian missile struck an oil refinery in the Israel city of Haifa.

stories.title: Iran’s strike on Qatar gas facility will reduce supply for 3 to 5 years
stories.description: Iran’s strike on Qatar’s Ras Laffan gas facility will cut about 17% of the country’s Liquefied Natural Gas exports.

stories.title: Qatar PM on Iran attacks: ‘Wisdom seems to be lacking these days’
stories.description: Qatar’s prime minister has said Iran’s claims that it is targeting US bases is “unacceptable and unjustified”.

stories.title: Who leads Iran? Assassinations leave leadership and command in question
stories.description: Analysts question Tehran’s power dynamics as younger hardliners may rise after killing of leaders Khamenei and Larijani.

stories.title: Israel says Haifa oil refinery hit in Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Israeli Energy Minister Eli Cohen says 'no significant damage' reported after Iranian strike on Haifa oil refinery.

stories.title: Trump references Pearl Harbor during meeting with Japanese PM on Iran war
stories.description: US president said that Japan was 'stepping up to the plate' to support the US war without offering details.

stories.title: Iran attacks cut 17% of Qatar’s LNG capacity for up to 5 years: QatarEnergy
stories.description: CEO Saad al-Kaabi says QatarEnergy may have to declare force majeure on long-term contracts for up to five years.

stories.title: Hegseth insists US is meeting Iran war objectives
stories.description: US Defense Secretary insisted Washington is meeting its objectives against Iran despite escalating attacks on Gulf.

stories.title: Hegseth says no ‘timeframe’ for war on Iran as Pentagon asks for $200bn
stories.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth hints war funding may change, leaving timeline decisions to President Trump.

stories.title: “It’s time to end the war.” UN’s Guterres urges US, Israel to cease attacks
stories.description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres warned the US and Israel of “potential tragic consequences” from the war in Iran.

stories.title: Child among survivors pulled from rubble of US-Israeli attack in Iran
stories.description: Video from Iran’s Red Crescent showed a child among survivors pulled from the wreckage of a US-Israeli air attack.

stories.title: China’s silence on Iran reveals its true priorities
stories.description: Beijing's muted response shows that when core interests are at stake, even close partners are expendable.

stories.title: UN’s Guterres tells US, Israel ‘it is high time to end war’, urges Iran to ‘stop attacking neighbours’
stories.description: United Nations Secretary General Antonio Guterres on Thursday called on the US and Isreal to end the war on Iran while urging Tehran to stop “attacking your neighbours”. “Let me start by two clear messages. First, to the United States and to Israel: It’s high time to end this war...

stories.title: European countries, Japan express ‘readiness’ to safeguard shipping through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Leaders from the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, and Japan have signalled their readiness to support efforts to ensure the safe passage of commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. “We express our readiness to contribute to appropriate efforts to e...

stories.title: Iran claims it struck US F-35 over central Iran, with fighter jet’s fate unclear
stories.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed on Thursday that it had struck and “seriously damaged” a US F-35 fighter jet over central Iran. In a statement, the IRGC said the aircraft was hit by its air defence systems at around 2:50am (local time) on Thursday (2320GMT...

stories.title: IMF raises concern over global inflation, output over US-Israel war on Iran
stories.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) said on Thursday it was monitoring the impacts of the US-Israel war on Iran on global inflation and output, but that no countries had so far approached it for emergency assistance related to the conflict. “If prolonged, higher energy prices w...

stories.title: Ministerial meeting calls on Iran to ‘immediately and unconditionally’ halt attacks in Gulf
stories.description: A ministerial meeting, hosted by Saudi Arabia, called on Iran to “immediately and unconditionally” halt attacks against Gulf countries in the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, the Saudi Arabian Foreign Ministry said on Thursday. The conflict began with US-Israeli strikes on Ir...

stories.title: ‘Baseless characterisation’: FO rejects remarks by Israel’s ambassador to India
stories.description: The Foreign Office (FO) on Thursday issued a statement rejecting the comments made by Israel’s ambassador to India, calling them a “baseless characterisation”. The FO statement referred to a video clip of the Israeli ambassador circulating on social media and said: “We firmly rej...

stories.title: FO rejects US intelligence chief’s claims about Pakistan missile threat
stories.description: The Foreign Office (FO) on Thursday categorically rejected the statement by US Dire­ctor of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard over Pakistan’s missile capabilities posing a threat to the country. Gabbard had listed Pakistan among states that pose a significant threat to the Unit...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
